title: DAAD Brussels Hiring Project Leader for EU–Africa Higher Education Initiative in Brussels
summary: The DAAD Brussels Office is now hiring a Project Leader for the EU-funded Study and Research in Africa: Opportunities (SRiA-O) initiative. This full-time role offers experienced international development and project management professionals the opportunity to lead a strategically significant EU–Africa cooperation initiative focused on higher education and research collaboration. Based in Brussels, the successful candidate will oversee implementation of a complex, multi-country project in partnership with the European Commission, African and European institutions, and international stakeholders. About the Role The Project Leader will be responsible for strategic and operational leadership of the SRiA-O project, ensuring effective implementation, stakeholder coordination, and accountability across multiple countries and institutions. This position is ideal for senior professionals with extensive experience managing transnational development or education projects, particularly within EU-funded frameworks. Key Responsibilities The selected candidate will: Lead strategic and operational management of the SRiA-O initiative Coordinate partners, subcontractors, and stakeholders across Europe and Africa Oversee governance processes including risk, quality, and change management Manage reporting, financial oversight, and accountability systems Support stakeholder engagement, communication, and external representation Candidate Profile Applicants should demonstrate: At least 10 years of experience managing transnational cooperation projects Strong experience with DG INTPA / EU-funded programmes Proven collaboration with African and European partner organizations Excellent coordination, drafting, and stakeholder engagement skills Fluency in English and French German language skills are considered an advantage Compensation and Benefits DAAD offers: Full-time employment (38 hours/week) Location: Brussels, Belgium Salary: €5,500–€6,000 gross per month (depending on experience) Attractive benefits package International and collaborative working environment Application Deadline Applications must be submitted by: 26 May 2026 How to Apply Submit your application to: buero.bruessel@daad.de Subject Line: SRiA-O Project Leader More details and full vacancy notice, Click Here For more job opportunities, Visit OFY here. The post DAAD Brussels Hiring Project Leader for EU–Africa Higher Education Initiative in Brussels appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Schuman Traineeship 2026–2027 at the European Parliament (EPRS): Paid EU Opportunity | Apply by 31 May
summary: The Schuman Traineeship Programme at the European Parliament is now open for applications. This highly competitive, paid traineeship offers graduates and selected vocational candidates the opportunity to gain hands-on experience in EU policymaking, research, and institutional work. If you are interested in international policy, governance, or parliamentary processes, this is one of the most prestigious entry points into EU careers. Key Details at a Glance Programme: Schuman Traineeship (EPRS – European Parliamentary Research Service) Institution: European Parliamentary Research Service (EPRS) Duration: October 2026 – February 2027 Application Deadline: 31 May (23:59 Brussels time) Location: European Parliament (various EU locations) Funding: Paid traineeship Application Limit: Maximum of 3 positions per candidate About the Programme The European Parliamentary Research Service (EPRS) provides independent research, analysis, and policy support to Members of the European Parliament and its committees. As a Schuman trainee, you will: Gain first-hand experience of EU institutional processes Work on real policy and research assignments Develop expertise in your chosen field Contribute to evidence-based policymaking Traineeship Areas Applicants can choose from a wide range of thematic and operational areas. Foreign and External Policies Latin America Europe Euromed and Middle East Transatlantic Relations and Global Governance Africa, Caribbean and Pacific Asia, Australia and New Zealand Parliamentary Committees Foreign Affairs (AFET) Human Rights (DROI) Security and Defence (SEDE) International Trade (INTA) Development (DEVE) External Policy Analysis Support Programme Focus Areas Election Observation Parliamentary Support and Capacity Building Human Rights Action Mediation and Dialogue Support Horizontal Services Communications and Outreach Strategy and Innovation Information Technology (IT) Human Resources Eligibility Criteria To apply for the Schuman traineeship, candidates must meet the following requirements: Be at least 18 years old Be a national of an EU Member State A limited number of positions are open to non-EU nationals Meet language requirements Hold: A university degree (EQF Level 6) , or A vocational qualification (EQF Levels 3–5) obtained within the EU (limited positions) Provide a valid criminal record certificate Not have: Worked or completed a traineeship in an EU institution for more than 2 months Conducted a study visit at the European Parliament Secretariat within the last 6 months New Update: Expanded Access The programme now includes expanded eligibility : A limited number of positions are open to candidates with vocational education qualifications (EQF Levels 3, 4, or 5) obtained within the EU This change broadens access beyond traditional university graduates Application Timeline October–February Traineeship Application Period: May Deadline: 31 May (23:59 Brussels time) Shortlisting: 1 July – 15 July Admissions: 1 July – 31 August Start Date: 1 October March–July Traineeship Application Period: October Deadline: 31 October Start Date: 1 March How to Apply The application process involves two main steps: 1. Register Visit the official traineeship portal Select a traineeship offer Create an account Complete your personal details 2. Submit Application Log in to your account Upload: CV (Europass format only) Motivation letter (maximum one page per application) Important Rules You may apply for up to 3 positions only Applying to more than 3 roles will result in disqualification Applications cannot be edited after submission No additional documents will be accepted via email Selection Process Shortlisting Conducted by relevant Directorates-General (DGs) Eligibility Verification Shortlisted candidates must upload: Passport or ID Academic or vocational certificates Criminal record Note: You have 14 days to upload documents after being shortlisted. Final Admission Offer is sent Candidate accepts Official admission letter issued Why Apply for the Schuman Traineeship? Gain direct exposure to EU policymaking Build a career pathway in international governance Work with leading experts in research, policy, and diplomacy Strengthen your profile for EU and global careers Application Link Apply here: https://ep-stages.gestmax.eu/website/application-informations Pro Tip for Applicants Use the Europass CV format correctly and clearly Tailor your motivation letter to each role Highlight: Research and analytical skills Policy or institutional experience Interest in EU affairs Final Thoughts The Schuman Traineeship at the European Parliament is a high-impact opportunity for individuals seeking careers in policy, governance, and international affairs. With expanded eligibility and diverse thematic areas, the programme is more accessible than ever. If you meet the criteria, ensure you apply before the 31 May deadline to secure your chance to work at the heart of the European Union. Application Link Apply here: https://ep-stages.gestmax.eu/website/application-informations The post Schuman Traineeship 2026–2027 at the European Parliament (EPRS): Paid EU Opportunity | Apply by 31 May appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNDP Paid Internships 2026 for Students and Graduates – Launch Your Global Development Career
summary: Are you passionate about world development and are looking for valuable experiences to complement your studies? The UNDP Internship Program is an outstanding opportunity where interns have the chance to learn about the leading development programs initiated by the United Nations. The UNDP Internship program aims to provide candidates interested in international development with a deeper knowledge of the UNDP’s work. The UNDP Internship is an excellent opportunity for individuals who are interested in hands-on experience with issues related to global poverty eradication and sustainable development. UNDP offers attractive paid positions in several areas for the sole purpose of promoting global development. Around 170 countries are part of the United Nations Development Program (UNDP), which is the leading development promotion agency of the UN. UNDP interns have a tremendous opportunity to gain valuable hands-on experience working with qualified, well-trained, and exceedingly experienced mentors and supervisors to achieve the goals of the UNDP. The organization works towards a better future for humanity by addressing issues such as eradicating poverty, reducing inequities and exclusion, and empowering countries to embark on sustainable development and growth. The UNDP internship program includes students and recent graduates from the most diverse fields of knowledge. The most popular areas of study in this program are Economics, Public or Business Administration, Environmental Studies, International Relations, and Other related areas. The type of activities that interns perform can vary depending on the opening and the needs of the department where they are placed. The following are the most common responsibilities: Conducting research, Writing documents, Cataloguing information, forums, or other collaborative events, Assisting in the organization of conferences, and Publishing knowledge stories/best practices. These internships can take place at the UN’s headquarters, its country offices, or regional offices. The length of an internship depends on both the candidate’s qualifications and the needs of the UN. UNDP Internship Program Details: Host Organization: United Nation Development Program Program Locations: UNDP Country Offices, at Regional Hubs or Headquarters in New York. Internship Type: Full-time/Part-time (Paid) Eligible Countries: All Deadline: Varies What are the Benefits of the UNDP Internship Program? The UNDP interns would receive a monthly stipend. UNDP interns receive a monthly stipend to cover costs associated with the internship. Stipend amounts can vary from one duty station to another. There are chances that interns will be assigned to UNDP or to the UN system in the future. Additionally, interns may receive financial aid from their nominating institutions or the government. Eligibility Criteria for the UNDP Internships: Applicants from all over the world may apply for the UNDP internships. Candidates can either be enrolled in a graduate program or have earned a second university degree, or they can be in the final academic year of their first university degree. The candidate should have fluency in at least one of UNDP’s three working languages: French, English, and Spanish. The knowledge of Arabic, Portuguese, Russian, or another Eastern European language would be advantageous. The candidates should be able to demonstrate their relevance to UNDP programs. At the time of application, the candidate must be at least 18 years of age. The candidate must demonstrate the ability to work with people from different languages, national, and cultural backgrounds. Deadline: The application deadline to apply for the UNDP internship varies according to each internship program. How to Apply for the UNDP Internship Program? Candidates must submit their applications only via UNDP’s website. Internship opportunities at UNDP are listed in the UNDP Job Section. Please select an area of interest and then click “Apply.”. Please ensure that you follow the specific deadline for the internship program, as each internship has a different and exclusive deadline. If you wish to apply to more than one program, you must register separately for each program. APPLY NOW (Search intern in the search bar) OFFICIAL WEBSITE (Search intern in the search bar) ✅️Several positions are now open at UNDP for Interns. Check out the opportunities below for international, local and remote personnel and with UNDP 💼 Explore this week’s vacancies: 💼 Explore this week’s vacancies: ➡️ Programme/project Intern (Paramaribo, Suriname) 📅 Jul-01-26 🔗 https://buff.ly/PlHrfO8 ➡️ Inclusive Governance and Gender Equality Intern (Luanda, Angola) 📅 May-14-26 🔗 https://buff.ly/VZrFfjF ➡️ ICT Intern (Harare, Zimbabwe) 📅 May-08-26 🔗 https://buff.ly/7PXkNdY ➡️ Business Operations Internship (Copenhagen, Denmark) 📅 May-10-26 🔗 https://buff.ly/VEhLHcV ➡️ Interns for projects / operations (Uzbekistan) 📅 May-11-26 🔗 https://buff.ly/I2nQMVL ➡️ Social Media Video Production Intern (New York, United States) 📅 May-16-26 🔗 https://buff.ly/cQy5ytY ➡️ Crisis Analysis and Coordination Intern (Beirut, Lebanon) 📅 May-04-26 🔗 https://buff.ly/MaQWQCj ➡️ AI Innovation Intern (New York, United States) 📅 May-10-26 🔗 https://buff.ly/wjCWNxP ➡️ Digital, AI and Innovation Internship (Home Based) 📅 Oct-01-26 🔗 https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/2026/04/28/digital-ai-and-innovation-ininternship-2026-undp-global-remote-opportunity-for-future-tech-and-policy-leadersrs/ The post UNDP Paid Internships 2026 for Students and Graduates – Launch Your Global Development Career appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNIDO Monitoring and Evaluation Specialist Job 2026 in Vienna: Full Guide to Eligibility, Responsibilities, Salary Insights & How to Apply
summary: The United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) is currently inviting applications for the role of Monitoring and Evaluation Specialist (ISA-P4 level) based in Vienna, Austria. This is a high-impact international consultancy opportunity for experienced professionals seeking to contribute to global development, sustainability, and industrial transformation. If you are looking to work within the United Nations system and play a key role in shaping monitoring, evaluation, and results-based management frameworks, this comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know. Overview of the UNIDO Monitoring and Evaluation Specialist Role Position Title: Monitoring and Evaluation Specialist Requisition ID: 7382 Grade: ISA-P4 Duty Station: Vienna, Austria Employment Type: Non-Staff Regular (International Consultant) Contract Duration: 6 Months Application Deadline: 12 May 2026 (11:59 PM Vienna Time) This position is open to both internal and external applicants , with a strong encouragement for female candidates and persons with disabilities to apply. About UNIDO and Its Global Mission UNIDO is a specialized agency of the United Nations dedicated to: Promoting inclusive and sustainable industrial development (ISID) Reducing poverty and hunger Supporting environmental sustainability Advancing global economic inclusion Its work aligns closely with the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development , particularly SDG 9 , which focuses on building resilient infrastructure, fostering innovation, and promoting sustainable industrialization. UNIDO’s strategic priorities under its Medium-Term Programme Framework (2026–2029) include: Sustainable global supply chains Renewable and clean energy Climate action and environmental sustainability Digital transformation and AI integration Gender equality and youth empowerment Circular economy and cleaner production Organizational Context of the Role This position is housed within: Directorate: Strategic Planning, Programming and Policy (SPP) Division: Strategic Programming, Results Monitoring and Quality Assurance (SPP/SPM) The role supports the implementation of UNIDO’s Monitoring and Reporting Policy , ensuring high-quality program delivery and measurable impact across global initiatives. Key Responsibilities of the Role As a Monitoring and Evaluation Specialist, you will play a central role in strengthening UNIDO’s results-based management systems. Core Duties Include: 1. Results-Based Management (RBM) System Enhancement Provide expert advice on reviewing and improving RBM frameworks Support institutional performance tracking systems 2. RBM Award Initiative Co-lead the design and implementation of UNIDO’s first RBM Award Identify and showcase best practices across projects and programs 3. Monitoring Evaluation Process Optimization Map and assess current M E systems Identify strengths, weaknesses, and cost implications Recommend improvements for efficiency and effectiveness 4. Data Collection and Reporting Support accurate and timely submission of program data Promote adherence to monitoring policies across departments 5. Tool and Template Development Design and refine M E tools, templates, and reporting systems Ensure alignment with updated frameworks 6. Corporate Reporting Contribute to key reports such as: Annual Reports Programme and Budget documents Medium-Term Programme Framework reports 7. Gender-Responsive Monitoring Integrate gender indicators and disaggregated data into reporting systems Promote gender equality in evaluation frameworks 8. Cross-Functional Collaboration Work with technical and non-technical teams Support organization-wide performance improvement Core Values and Competencies Expected UNIDO Core Values Integrity Professionalism Respect for diversity Key Competencies Strong collaboration and teamwork Results-driven mindset Effective communication and trust-building Innovation and problem-solving Eligibility Criteria and Requirements Educational Qualifications Advanced university degree (Master’s or equivalent) in: Economics Environmental Management Engineering Or a related field Professional Experience Minimum 7 years of relevant experience At least 3 years at the international level Proven experience in: Monitoring and Evaluation Technical cooperation projects Results-Based Management (desirable) Gender equality and empowerment initiatives Experience working within: International organizations Preferably the United Nations system Language Requirements Fluency in English (written and spoken) Knowledge of another UNIDO working language is required Additional languages are an advantage Important Application Information Applications must be submitted online only Shortlisted candidates will be contacted Selected candidates may be required to disclose financial interests Official Application Link APPLY HERE Important Notes for Applicants UNIDO does not charge any application fees Be cautious of fraudulent recruitment offers The appointment is temporary and project-based There is no guarantee of contract renewal Why You Should Apply This role offers a unique opportunity to: Career Benefits Work within the United Nations system Gain international exposure Contribute to global development and sustainability Professional Growth Strengthen expertise in monitoring and evaluation Engage in high-level policy and program design Collaborate with global stakeholders Impact Influence programs aligned with Sustainable Development Goals Support gender equality and inclusive growth Final Thoughts The UNIDO Monitoring and Evaluation Specialist position in Vienna is a prestigious and impactful opportunity for experienced professionals looking to contribute to global development at a strategic level. With a strong focus on results-based management, gender inclusion, and sustainable development, this role is ideal for candidates who are passionate about creating measurable impact through data-driven decision-making. If you meet the qualifications and are ready to take your career to the international stage, this is an opportunity worth pursuing. Click HERE to view more details about this career offer. Discover more career opportunities on HERE . title: Sharjah Film Platform 2026 Short Film Production Grant: Up to $30,000 Funding for Filmmakers Worldwide (Eligibility, Deadline & How to Apply)
summary: The Sharjah Art Foundation has officially opened applications for the c , a highly competitive and globally recognized funding opportunity designed to support independent filmmakers in completing innovative short films. This grant is part of the Foundation’s flagship Sharjah Film Platform (SFP) —an annual festival dedicated to celebrating independent cinema and pushing the boundaries of contemporary moving image storytelling. If you are an emerging or established filmmaker seeking funding, exposure, and a global premiere platform, this comprehensive guide provides everything you need to know. What is the Sharjah Film Platform Short Film Production Grant? The SFP Short Film Production Grant is an international funding opportunity aimed at supporting the completion of short films that demonstrate originality, creativity, and strong storytelling. The grant is specifically designed to: Encourage bold, experimental, and impactful filmmaking Support independent filmmakers globally Expand the boundaries of cinematic expression Provide a platform for showcasing completed works Selected filmmakers will not only receive financial support but also gain the opportunity to premiere their films at a future edition of the Sharjah Film Platform , significantly boosting their visibility in the global film industry. Grant Value and Funding Details Total Grant Pool: 120,000 AED (approximately $30,000 USD) Number of Awardees: Varies depending on project needs Individual Grant Amount: Determined based on the scope and budget of each project This flexible funding model ensures that projects receive support tailored to their production requirements. Application Deadline Deadline: 28 May 2026 Time: 11:59 PM (UAE Time, GMT +4) Late submissions will not be accepted under any circumstances, so applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early. Who Can Apply? The grant is open to: Independent filmmakers from anywhere in the world Applicants of all ages and backgrounds Creators working across any film genre There are no geographical or thematic restrictions, making this one of the most inclusive film grants globally. What Qualifies as a Short Film? For this grant: A short film must have a maximum runtime of 50 minutes , including credits Both narrative and experimental formats are accepted What the Grant Supports Funding is primarily intended for: Film production Post-production processes Technical and creative services, including: Filming and editing Sound design and music production Image and sound licensing Translation and subtitling Applicants must clearly outline how the funds will be allocated in their budget plan. Application Requirements To successfully apply, candidates must submit the following materials: Mandatory Documents Completed application form Three-minute recorded pitch (via Vimeo or YouTube) Original script Detailed budget plan Production schedule Failure to submit all required materials will result in automatic disqualification . Key Application Rules Only one application per director is allowed Multiple submissions will lead to disqualification (only the first will be considered) Applications must be submitted before the deadline Film Script Language Requirements Script Language: English or Arabic Final Film Language: Any language is allowed Subtitles: Must include both Arabic and English subtitles What Makes a Strong Application? Applications are evaluated based on: 1. Creative Vision Clarity of the film’s concept Strength of storytelling and structure 2. Originality Unique script and narrative approach 3. Technical Artistic Quality Demonstration of filmmaking skills 4. Feasibility Realistic production schedule and budget Understanding the 3-Minute Pitch Requirement The pitch is a crucial part of your application and should: Clearly communicate your film idea Highlight your vision and storytelling style Showcase your creativity and personality You may: Speak directly to the camera Present visuals or trailers Share previous work Ownership and Rights Filmmakers retain full copyright and distribution rights Grant recipients must acknowledge the Sharjah Art Foundation in film credits Eligibility Restrictions Only unfinished films are eligible Previously completed or screened films cannot be submitted Selection and Results Timeline Applications are reviewed by a selection committee Results will be announced by the end of 2026 Past Grant Recipients and Impact Previous editions of the SFP grant have supported a wide range of international filmmakers, resulting in critically recognized projects across diverse themes and regions. This demonstrates the program’s strong track record in: Supporting emerging talent Promoting global storytelling Advancing independent cinema Why You Should Apply This grant offers a rare combination of funding, exposure, and creative freedom: Key Benefits Access to up to $30,000 in funding Opportunity to premiere your film at an international festival Global recognition and industry exposure Creative independence with full ownership rights Support for production and post-production How to Apply Step-by-Step Process Prepare all required materials Record your 3-minute pitch Complete the application form Submit before the deadline Application Link Apply here Final Thoughts The Sharjah Film Platform 2026 Short Film Production Grant stands out as one of the most accessible and impactful funding opportunities for filmmakers worldwide. With no restrictions on nationality or genre, and a strong emphasis on creativity and originality, it offers a powerful platform for filmmakers to bring their vision to life. If you are ready to take your film project to the next level, this grant is an opportunity you should not miss. For more information about this grant visit here Discover more grant opportunities on OFY here The post Sharjah Film Platform 2026 Short Film Production Grant: Up to $30,000 Funding for Filmmakers Worldwide (Eligibility, Deadline How to Apply) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNDP Young Professional Opportunity 2026 in India: Launch Your Career in Inclusive Value Chains and Sustainable Development
summary: If you re passionate about sustainable development, economic empowerment, and making a real-world impact, the Young Professional – Inclusive Value Chains opportunity with United Nations Development Programme in India offers an exceptional career pathway. This 12-month full-time assignment places you at the heart of development initiatives that directly support marginalized communities, especially women and rural populations. This detailed guide explores everything you need to know—from responsibilities and benefits to eligibility and application timelines—so you can confidently apply and stand out. About UNDP and Its Mission in India The United Nations Development Programme is a global leader in sustainable development, operating in over 170 countries. Its mission is centered on: Eradicating poverty Reducing inequalities Strengthening democratic governance Promoting climate resilience In India, UNDP has been active since 1951, working closely with the government and development partners to implement impactful programs across all states and union territories. UNDP India’s Strategic Focus (2023–2027) The current Country Programme emphasizes: Strong institutions for achieving Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Inclusive economic growth and social protection systems Climate-smart and resilient development solutions This Young Professional role directly contributes to Sustainable Development Goal 10: Reduced Inequalities . Position Overview: Young Professional – Inclusive Value Chains Location: New Delhi, India Duration: 12 months (with possible extension) Start Date: 01 June 2026 Work Type: Full-time, onsite Positions Available: 4 Host Organization: UNDP Volunteer Category: Associate UN Volunteer This role is part of a major initiative: Market-Led Inclusive Value Chain Development Programme , designed to empower rural communities through sustainable livelihoods and economic participation. Key Responsibilities As a Young Professional, you will play a vital role in program coordination and implementation. Your responsibilities include: Programme Coordination and Implementation Assist in planning and executing activities across various value chains Track progress and ensure timely delivery of outputs Collaborate with implementing partners Stakeholder Engagement Work with government bodies, private sector partners, and community organizations Facilitate coordination with State Rural Livelihood Missions (SRLMs) Event and Workshop Management Organize meetings, consultations, and training sessions Prepare reports, materials, and documentation Monitoring and Reporting Maintain data trackers and compile progress reports Support preparation of briefs and summaries Knowledge Management Document case studies, best practices, and lessons learned Promote information sharing across stakeholders Focus Areas of the Programme The initiative targets transformative impact in: Women’s economic empowerment Youth innovation and entrepreneurship Skills development and job creation Social protection systems Inclusive and responsible business practices Special emphasis is placed on integrating women into value chains—not just as participants, but as leaders and decision-makers. Benefits and Allowances Participants receive a comprehensive support package, including: Monthly Allowance: INR 56,126.69 Entry Lump Sum: USD 350 Relocation Support: USD 175 (if applicable) Exit Allowance: USD 120 per month served Insurance Coverage: Health, life, and safety Annual Leave: 2.5 days per month Learning Opportunities: Access to UN training resources Eligibility Criteria Education Bachelor’s degree in fields such as: Rural Development Economics Business Administration Agriculture / Agribusiness Public Policy Social Sciences Experience Minimum 2 years of relevant experience in development programmes, livelihoods, or value chain initiatives Skills and Competencies Strong communication and reporting skills Ability to work with diverse stakeholders Proficiency in MS Office tools Teamwork, adaptability, and professionalism Language Requirements English (Fluent – Required) Hindi (Working knowledge – Desirable) Additional Requirements Must be a national or legal resident of India Living in New Delhi New Delhi offers a vibrant mix of tradition and modernity. As a global city, it provides: Diverse cultural experiences Extensive professional networking opportunities Exposure to large-scale development projects However, candidates should be prepared to arrange their own accommodation and adapt to a fast-paced urban environment. Why You Should Apply This opportunity is ideal if you want to: Build a career with the United Nations system Work on high-impact development projects Gain hands-on experience in inclusive economic growth Contribute to gender equality and social inclusion Develop global professional networks Application Deadline 13 May 2026 Make sure to submit your application before the deadline to be considered for this competitive opportunity. How to Apply Interested candidates should apply through the official UN Volunteer platform: Visit the UNV portal Search for “Young Professional – Inclusive Value Chains” Complete your profile and submit your application For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Volunteering Opportunities on OFY here . title: Support to Human Rights, Democracy and Civil Society in Jordan 2026 Grants Offer Up to €1 Million for NGOs
summary: The Support to Human Rights, Democracy and Civil Society in Jordan 2026 call for proposals is now open, offering grants of up to €1 million for eligible non-profit organisations. This major European Union funding opportunity is designed for civil society organisations, NGOs, and development actors working to strengthen democratic participation, human rights, media freedom, and local development in Jordan. With a total budget of €3 million , the programme provides significant support for organisations ready to scale their impact, build stronger partnerships, and contribute to inclusive social progress. For organisations seeking substantial funding to deliver long-term initiatives in Jordan, this is an opportunity worth serious attention. About the Program The call is managed by the European Commission and aims to support an independent, participatory, and empowered civil society in Jordan. It is divided into two funding lots, each targeting an important area of development. Lot 1: Support to Media and Freedom of Expression has an indicative allocation of €2 million . This lot focuses on strengthening independent media, protecting freedom of expression, and supporting open civic space. Lot 2: Civil Society Support to Local Development has an indicative allocation of €1 million . It is intended to strengthen civil society organisations as contributors to governance, development planning, and community-level progress. Successful proposals must take place in Jordan and run for 24 to 36 months . Why This Opportunity Matters Civil society organisations often face funding constraints when trying to deliver sustainable, large-scale impact. This programme offers not only financial support but also strategic backing from the European Union for organisations working in areas essential to democratic development. For Jordan-based organisations, this can help expand services, strengthen advocacy, improve community engagement, and create long-term institutional capacity. For international organisations partnering with local actors, the programme offers a valuable route to collaborative and locally grounded development work. At a time when civic participation, accountable governance, and inclusive development remain global priorities, this funding can help organisations turn ideas into measurable action. What Participants Will Gain Successful applicants can gain far more than grant funding through this programme. First, organisations can access substantial financial support , with grants between €900,000 and €1,000,000 . This level of funding enables organisations to implement ambitious multi-year projects with real scale. Second, applicants can strengthen their institutional credibility by partnering on an EU-funded initiative. This can improve future fundraising prospects and increase visibility among donors and stakeholders. Third, the programme encourages partnership-building between local and international organisations, creating opportunities for knowledge exchange and long-term collaboration. Finally, funded organisations can generate lasting social impact by supporting rights-based initiatives, stronger communities, and more inclusive governance systems. Who Can Apply Eligible lead applicants must be: Legal persons Non-profit organisations Non-governmental organisations or similar civil society entities Effectively established in Jordan or an EU Member State Applicants may apply individually or together with co-applicants and affiliated entities, provided they meet the programme requirements. Organisations should also demonstrate the ability to directly manage projects and deliver results. Eligible Areas Projects funded under this programme must take place in Jordan . The initiative is especially relevant for organisations working in: Human rights Media development Freedom of expression Civic participation Governance reform Community development Youth engagement Capacity building for local organisations Application Process and Deadline This is a restricted call for proposals. In the first stage, applicants must submit a concept note through the European Commission’s PROSPECT online portal. Shortlisted organisations will then be invited to submit a full application. Applicants are encouraged to prepare early, review eligibility criteria carefully, and ensure organisational registrations are completed in advance. Application Deadline: 14 June 2026 (12:00 Brussels time) Learn more and apply on the official European Commission website . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: AEA Graduate Education Diversity Internship (GEDI) Program 2026–2027: Paid $15,000 Internship for Graduate Students in Research and Evaluation
summary: The AEA Graduate Education Diversity Internship (GEDI) Program 2026–2027 is a paid professional development opportunity for graduate students in the United States who want to build careers in research, evaluation, and culturally responsive practice. Offered by the American Evaluation Association , the program provides a $15,000 stipend , hands-on internship experience, mentorship, training seminars, and access to a national network of evaluation professionals. Designed to support students from historically underrepresented backgrounds, GEDI helps emerging scholars strengthen their research skills while gaining practical experience in the growing field of evaluation. About the Program The GEDI Program runs from September 2026 through July 2027 and combines academic learning with applied professional experience. Scholars complete the equivalent of approximately two days per week at an internship placement, usually located near their graduate institution. Interns work with agencies and organizations on real evaluation projects that may involve assessing programs, analyzing outcomes, measuring community impact, or supporting evidence-based decision-making. These placements are structured to help participants apply their graduate training to real-world challenges. In addition to the internship placement, scholars participate in seminars, webinars, networking events, and national conference activities throughout the academic year. Why This Opportunity Matters Many graduate students build strong research skills but have limited exposure to evaluation as a career path. The GEDI Program bridges that gap by helping students transfer academic research knowledge into professional evaluation practice. Evaluation professionals play a critical role across sectors including public health, education, government, nonprofits, philanthropy, and social impact organizations. They help institutions understand what works, what needs improvement, and how programs can better serve communities. GEDI also places a strong emphasis on culturally responsive evaluation, helping scholars think critically about race, culture, equity, language, and inclusion in research design and practice. This makes the program especially valuable for students interested in ethical and community-centered careers. What Participants Will Gain Selected scholars receive a $15,000 stipend in recognition of their internship work and successful completion of program requirements. Major travel costs for required in-person components, including airfare and shared hotel accommodations, are also reimbursed. Participants gain direct evaluation experience through a supervised internship placement connected to their field of interest. They also receive mentorship from experienced professionals, guidance from program leadership, and support from an academic advisor. The program includes training seminars in Washington, D.C., attendance at the Eval2026 Conference in Charlotte, North Carolina , monthly webinars, and networking opportunities with leaders in evaluation. By the end of the program, scholars will have practical work experience, professional connections, stronger applied research skills, and a clearer pathway into careers in evaluation and impact measurement. Who Can Apply The GEDI Program is open to students who are currently enrolled in a master’s or doctoral program in the United States and have completed at least one full year of graduate-level coursework. Applicants should have prior exposure to research methods and demonstrate a strong interest in applying research skills to evaluation. Candidates must also show commitment to culturally responsive practice and be eligible to work for pay in the United States. The program is particularly focused on supporting graduate students of color and individuals from historically underrepresented backgrounds. Eligible Areas Applicants must be residing in the United States during the program period and enrolled in a U.S.-based graduate institution. Students from a wide range of disciplines are encouraged to apply, including public health, education, sociology, psychology, anthropology, political science, social work, and related fields. Application Process and Deadline Applicants should prepare essays, a professional reference letter, academic advisor support, and a CV as part of the application process. Finalists may be invited for interviews. The deadline to apply for the GEDI Program 2026–2027 is 15 May 2026 . Graduate students interested in paid professional experience, mentorship, and a pathway into evaluation careers should strongly consider applying. Learn more and apply on the official AEA website . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Conservation Futures Intern in South Africa 2026 (Paid Internship with Arizona State University & Conservation International)
summary: If you are passionate about environmental sustainability, wildlife conservation, and community development, the Conservation Futures Internship 2026 offers a rare and transformative opportunity to gain hands-on experience in one of Africa’s most important ecological landscapes. Organized by Conservation International in partnership with Arizona State University , this immersive internship combines fieldwork, academic learning, and professional development in South Africa. Set within the breathtaking Kruger to Canyons Landscape , this program places interns at the heart of real-world conservation challenges and solutions—making it an ideal stepping stone for future conservation leaders. About the Conservation Futures Internship The Conservation Futures Internship is a three-month, paid, full-time field-based program designed to equip students with practical experience in conservation and community engagement. Location Acornhoek, Limpopo Province, South Africa Near the iconic Kruger National Park Internship Duration Start Date: June 1, 2026 End Date: August 31, 2026 Application Deadline May 15, 2026 (11:49 PM EST) What Makes This Internship Unique? This is not a typical internship—it is a deeply immersive, field-driven experience that blends science, culture, and community. Key Highlights Paid Internship: Financial support provided Travel Support: In-country travel expenses covered Free Accommodation: Housing near the work site Transport Provided: Daily commute arranged Global Certification: Certificate from Arizona State University upon completion Online Courses: Free academic training alongside fieldwork Mentorship: Guidance from experienced conservation professionals Work You Will Be Doing Interns will actively contribute to conservation and community-based initiatives. Responsibilities may vary depending on the project site but typically include: 1. Conservation Field Support Assist in wildlife monitoring and habitat restoration Participate in planting and ecological restoration activities Conduct field assessments and biodiversity tracking Support conservation agreements with local stakeholders 2. Community Engagement Work closely with local and Indigenous communities Support workshops, meetings, and outreach programs Document how conservation aligns with community livelihoods 3. Research Documentation Collect and organize field data Capture real-life conservation stories through writing and visuals Contribute to reports, presentations, and learning materials 4. Innovation Communication Help develop creative solutions for conservation challenges Collaborate with teams to improve outcomes for both people and nature 5. Professional Development Participate in mentorship sessions and cohort learning Reflect on personal and professional growth Build cross-cultural and fieldwork skills Working Conditions This internship is designed for individuals ready for a real-world experience: Significant time spent outdoors in rural and remote environments Exposure to basic living conditions Frequent interaction with local communities Local travel within the project area (transport provided) Learning Experience with Arizona State University A standout feature of this program is the integration of academic learning: Interns will complete online courses through Arizona State University’s School of Conservation Futures Courses are fully funded (free of charge) Upon successful completion, participants receive a professional certificate This dual approach ensures that interns gain both practical skills and theoretical knowledge . Eligibility Criteria Required Qualifications Enrollment at Arizona State University Academic background in: Conservation Environmental Studies Community Development Or related fields Strong interest in real-world conservation Willingness to live and work in remote areas Cultural sensitivity and teamwork skills Adaptability and a proactive mindset Preferred Qualifications Experience in: Fieldwork Community engagement Volunteer programs Skills in: Ecology Natural resource management Communications or storytelling Interest in: Wildlife conservation Habitat restoration Human-wildlife relationships Familiarity with Southern African contexts (an advantage) How To Apply. Click Here for more and to apply Applications are reviewed on merit, and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Why You Should Apply This internship is ideal for students who want to: Gain hands-on conservation experience Work in one of Africa’s most biodiverse regions Learn directly from experts and local communities Build a global professional network Develop skills that are highly valued in sustainability careers Beyond the professional benefits, this experience offers a chance to make a tangible impact on both environmental conservation and community well-being. Equal Opportunity Commitment Conservation International is committed to fair and inclusive hiring practices , ensuring equal opportunity for all applicants regardless of background, in alignment with South African employment equity laws. For more global internship opportunities Click here . title: Royal Academy of Engineering Policy Fellowships 2026
summary: The Royal Academy of Engineering (RAEng) Policy Fellowships Programme is one of the most prestigious professional development opportunities designed to bridge the gap between engineering expertise and public policymaking. This fellowship equips policymakers with advanced technical insights, systems-thinking skills, and access to a powerful interdisciplinary network of engineers, researchers, and industry experts. The programme is structured to help policymakers tackle complex national and global challenges by integrating engineering solutions into policy frameworks. It is especially valuable in today’s rapidly evolving technological landscape, where policymaking increasingly requires technical literacy and innovation-driven approaches. According to official information, the programme fosters a “unique network of policymakers, engineers and other experts” working collaboratively to develop solutions to economic, social, and technical challenges. Fellowship Overview and Structure The RAEng Policy Fellowship runs over a four-month intensive programme, combining practical learning, expert engagement, and policy-focused research. Core Programme Components Participants benefit from: One-on-one meetings with leading engineers and subject-matter experts Systems-thinking coaching sessions Workshops (both in-person and virtual) Peer-to-peer collaboration sessions Development of a personal policy-focused report Presentation of findings to a cohort of fellows Additionally, fellows join a long-term alumni network, gaining continued access to professional development opportunities, events, and collaborations. Who Can Apply? (Eligible Applicants) Target Audience This fellowship is specifically designed for: Policymakers and public servants Professionals involved in public policy design or service delivery Individuals working in organizations with a public service mission Geographic Eligibility Primarily UK-based applicants Applicants must be working within the UK policy ecosystem or public service sector This means that applicants from Africa, Asia, Europe, or other regions are not directly eligible unless they are currently based in the UK and working in a relevant policy role. Qualifications and Requirements To be considered competitive, applicants must meet the following criteria: 1. Professional Role and Impact Must be actively involved in policymaking or public service Should have the ability to influence policy decisions or implementation 2. Defined Policy Challenge Applicants must present a clear and well-defined policy problem or technical challenge that can benefit from engineering insights 3. Application Vision Must demonstrate how the fellowship will be applied to real-world policy impact Should show clear expectations and learning outcomes 4. Employer Support (Mandatory) Applicants must have: A sponsoring manager Employer commitment to fund participation Self-funding is not allowed 5. Commitment to Programme Must actively participate throughout the fellowship Expected to contribute as a long-term alumni member Key Benefits of the Fellowship 1. High-Level Networking Participants connect with: Leading engineers Academic experts Industry professionals 2. Policy Innovation Skills The programme enhances: Systems thinking Problem-solving using engineering frameworks Evidence-based policymaking 3. Career Advancement Fellows gain: Recognition in policy and engineering circles Access to exclusive alumni opportunities Invitations to high-level events and collaborations 4. Real-World Impact Participants develop actionable solutions to real policy challenges, often influencing national or institutional decision-making. Application Process (Step-by-Step) Step 1: Register Online Applicants must create a profile via the RAEng grant management system. Step 2: Review Guidance Carefully read the FAQ and application guidance materials before submission. Step 3: Prepare Application Include: Defined policy challenge Learning objectives Employer endorsement Step 4: Submit Application Applications are submitted through the official online system. Step 5: Optional Support Sessions Applicants can attend weekly drop-in sessions for: Application guidance Feedback on policy challenges Apply here Important Notes for Applicants This is a highly competitive fellowship with strict selection criteria Employer sponsorship is non-negotiable The programme is not open for independent or self-funded applicants Strong applications clearly link engineering expertise to policy impact Why This Fellowship Matters The RAEng Policy Fellowships Programme is not just another professional course—it is a transformative opportunity for policymakers to rethink how policies are designed and implemented in a technology-driven world. By integrating engineering principles into governance, fellows gain the tools needed to address: Climate challenges Infrastructure development Digital transformation Public health systems Economic resilience Deadline 20th May 2026 If you are a policymaker working in the UK and seeking to elevate your impact through technical expertise and interdisciplinary collaboration, the Royal Academy of Engineering Policy Fellowship is a powerful opportunity. However, due to its geographic restriction, international applicants (including those from Africa) must first secure a UK-based policy role to become eligible.
